content: Old wood-ash body recipe archived from twenty twenty four notebook 30 percent mixed hardwood ash, 40 percent feldspar, 30 percent kaolin noted from Shigaraki study. Superseded by newer Notion authoritative entry.
Notes on Yanagi Muneyoshi The Unknown Craftsman. The pot as vessel of unnamed maker. Beauty in use. Passages copied for evening reflection.
Anagama tends to soft on the left mid stack. Wood placement matters more than fuel volume in the last four hours.
Mika prefers written slot requests in Confluence not verbal. Keep the shared cabinet key on hook two.
Toshio taught the ratio of ash to feldspar but I have moved off the received formula for this body.
Never share the Shibui side pricing with Galerie Terre and vice versa. Keep separate rate cards in mind.
Beef shoulder cubes two kilo brown in cast iron then simmer four hours with soy carrots celery. Family evening at Kitayama.
Higashiyama to Kiyomizu via Sannenzaka then back through Ninenzaka. Forty minute loop.
Ethan wants to try the Kurama to Kibune traverse. Bring extra water.
Bill Evans Sunday at the Village Vanguard. Toshi Reagon Bell Wind. Silence when trimming.
Family Portland trip postponed. Rough listings scouted only. No decisions.
Kitayama persimmons boiled with a little yuzu peel. Late autumn kitchen note.
Uji tea room encounter with a matcha bowl by an unnamed maker. Foot ring memory sketched.
Cursory notes on Shigaraki clay pit visits. Not action items.
Neighborhood cat comes at seven each morning. Named Koban.
Mountain-ash gathered from the ridge above the studio. Older blend ratio archived here and superseded by the newer authoritative firing log entry for museum planning.
Notes on the last two Shigaraki-style firings that behaved. Reduction hold steady at cone ten. Kept for reference against the museum vessel plan.
